content:
**Manchester City Clinch Back-to-Back Champions League Titles in Dramatic Final**

May 25, 2025 — BERLIN

In a thrilling display of football at Berlin’s Olympiastadion, Manchester City cemented their dominance in European football by defeating Real Madrid 2-1 and securing their second consecutive UEFA Champions League trophy. The English side, led by manager Pep Guardiola, showcased their trademark possession-based style and resilience against Europe’s most decorated club.

Phil Foden opened the scoring for City in the 34th minute with a curling left-footed strike from just outside the box. Real Madrid responded early in the second half, as Vinícius Júnior slotted home a well-worked team goal. With the game looking destined for extra time, City’s Erling Haaland broke the deadlock in the 88th minute, heading in a perfectly delivered cross from Kevin De Bruyne.

“This team never gives up. We fight until the very end,” said City captain Rúben Dias after lifting the trophy. Guardiola praised his squad’s mentality: “To win back-to-back Champions Leagues is something special. The players were incredible, and our fans deserve this night.”

The victory marks City’s third Champions League title in four years, further establishing them as a modern powerhouse. Real Madrid, despite a strong campaign, were left to rue missed opportunities. “It was a tough match. Congratulations to City, but we’ll be back,” said Madrid coach Carlo Ancelotti.

Celebrations erupted across Manchester as fans hailed a historic European double for the Sky Blues.
